← Text Tools

Text Difference Checker

Paste two versions of your text side by side and see exactly what changed — additions in green, deletions in red.

Differences

Paste text in both panels above to see differences.

How to compare two texts online

Paste your original text in the left panel and your revised version in the right panel. The differences appear instantly below — no button press required. Added lines are highlighted in green, removed lines in red, and unchanged lines are shown in their original color.

What is a text diff?

A diff (short for difference) is a comparison output that shows exactly which lines, words or characters changed between two versions of a text. The concept originates from software version control — developers use diff tools daily to review code changes before merging. The same technique is useful for tracking edits to any text: articles, contracts, scripts, emails and more.

Who uses a text difference checker?

Writers comparing drafts to see what an editor changed. Developers reviewing code changes without a full IDE. Legal teams verifying that only approved edits were made to contracts. Students checking which parts of their essay were flagged for revision. Translators comparing the source text against a reference translation. SEO professionals auditing changes to page copy or metadata.

Line diff vs character diff — what's better?

Line-level diffs (comparing line by line) are better for code and structured text where lines have semantic meaning. Character or word-level diffs are better for prose where a single sentence may change across multiple lines. This tool operates at the line level, which gives clear, readable output for most document comparison tasks.

Is my text safe?

Yes. Both text inputs never leave your browser. All comparison logic runs locally using JavaScript. Nothing is transmitted to any server.
